articles.title: Punjab PDMA warns of heatwave from May 7 to 11 - Dunya News
articles.full_text:
Summary Punjab PDMA warns of heatwave from May 7-11, temperatures up to 47°C expected, citizens advised precautions and emergency services alerted.
LAHORE (Dunya News) - The Provincial Disaster Management Authority Punjab has forecast a severe heatwave across the province from May 7 to 11.
According to the authority, temperatures in upper Punjab, including Lahore, Rawalpindi, Attock, Jhelum and Sargodha, are expected to range between 39°C and 43°C, with a noticeable rise in heat levels.
In southern Punjab, temperatures may climb between 43°C and 47°C, affecting areas such as Dera Ghazi Khan, Bahawalpur and Rahim Yar Khan.
Punjab Relief Commissioner Nabeel Javed has directed commissioners and deputy commissioners to remain alert, warning that even nighttime temperatures will stay above normal.
He cautioned that intense heat could trigger dust storms and urged children, women and elderly citizens to take precautionary measures.
Citizens have been advised to avoid unnecessary outdoor activities, stay hydrated, while farmers have been instructed to protect crops and livestock in advance.
The commissioner also directed Rescue 1122 to remain on high alert to assist heatstroke and dehydration victims, and ensure relief camps, clean drinking water, ORS and first aid facilities in urban areas.
PDMA added that mobile health teams will remain active to handle heat-related cases, while rising temperatures may accelerate snowmelt in northern regions.

articles.title: Delhi CM Flags Off Mobile Heat Relief Units Amid Record Heatwave - english.punjabkesari.com
articles.full_text:
Delhi CM Rekha Gupta Release Mobile Heat Relief Units: As a punishing heatwave continues to grip the national capital, Chief Minister Smt. Rekha Gupta today flagged off 13 specialized Mobile Heat Relief Units from the Delhi Secretariat. These units are designed to serve as a frontline defense for citizens—particularly outdoor workers and the homeless—vulnerable to the soaring temperatures. Accompanied by the release of a comprehensive “Heat Action Plan Booklet,” the initiative aims to bridge the gap between emergency services and those on the ground.
By deploying these mobile stations directly to high-traffic areas, the government seeks to mitigate the rising cases of heat exhaustion and sunstroke. Officials emphasized that this localized approach is critical for a city currently navigating one of its most severe climatic challenges in recent years.
#WATCH दिल्ली: दिल्ली की मुख्यमंत्री रेखा गुप्ता ने कहा, "दिल्ली में लगातार हीट एक्शन प्लान पर काम चल रहा है। आज दिल्ली सरकार ने अपना हीट एक्शन प्लान जारी किया है। इस पर एक किताब भी जारी की गई है। 13 जिलों के लिए 13 गाड़ियां रवाना की गई हैं जो इस हीट के साथ जंग करेंगी…… pic.twitter.com/jA51kwdlhN
— ANI_HindiNews (@AHindinews) May 6, 2026
On-Ground Support and Infrastructure
The newly deployed units are more than just transport vehicles; they function as mobile cooling stations equipped to handle the immediate physiological impacts of extreme heat. Strategically positioned across Delhi’s busiest intersections and labor hubs, each unit is stocked with:
-
Hydration Essentials: Continuous supplies of chilled, clean drinking water and ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) packets to combat electrolyte imbalance.
-
Medical Aid: First Aid kits and basic medical support specifically tailored for heat-related illnesses like syncopes or heat cramps.
-
Protective Gear: Distribution of cotton gamchas (traditional scarves) and caps to help citizens shield themselves from direct UV exposure.
The Heat Action Plan
Beyond immediate relief, the Chief Minister highlighted the importance of long-term resilience through the Heat Action Plan Booklet. This document outlines a structured protocol for various government departments to synchronize their efforts, ensuring that water tankers, cooling centers, and hospital emergency wards are operating at peak capacity.
The booklet also serves as an educational tool for the public, detailing how to recognize early symptoms of heatstroke and providing tips on maintaining domestic cooling without excessive energy consumption.
Safety Guidelines for Citizens
With the mercury showing no signs of a significant drop, the Delhi government has issued a stern advisory for all residents:
-
Hydrate Constantly: Drink water even if you do not feel thirsty.
-
Peak Hour Caution: Avoid direct sun exposure between 12:00 PM and 4:00 PM.
-
Emergency Contact: For any heat-related medical emergencies, citizens are urged to contact the 112 helpline immediately for rapid response and transport to the nearest medical facility.
